title: VimFu — Ctrl-I / Tab
description: Ctrl-I (same as Tab) jumps to the newer position in the jump list. Opposite of Ctrl-O — go forward after going back.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

steps.say: Control I jumps forward in the jump list. It's the opposite of control O. Tab does the same thing.

steps.comment: --- Demo 1: jump around, then navigate back and forward ---

steps.say: First, let's create some jumps. Go to the bottom, then search for shields.
Now we have two jumps in history. Control O to go back.
Back at line one. Now control I to go forward again.
Forward one jump. We're at the bottom where we pressed G.
Forward to the Shields search. Control O and control I bounce between all your jump points.
Control I or tab. Jump forward. O goes back, I goes forward.
